dataset.name: MedNorm: A Corpus and Embeddings for Cross-terminology Medical Concept Normalisation
dataset.authors: Maksim Belousov; William G. Dixon; Goran Nenadic
dataset.mirror_note: A third-party mirror may be used only as a transport convenience; it does not override the official licence.

baseline.candidate_space: TRAIN-derived MedDRA code aliases; closed-code diagnostic
baseline.oracle_warning: This is only the upper bound if a human always selects the gold code whenever it appears in Top-K; it is NOT observed human-assisted accuracy.

deepseek.rationale_validation_rule: Every accepted DeepSeek candidate rationale must cite at least one exact allowed real-data phrase and preserve the fixed code set. When DeepSeek is unavailable, every option still carries deterministic grounded evidence/rationale and is explicitly labelled as such.

evidence_boundary: Real phrases and human reference codes come from the public MedNorm-derived data. Candidate terminology is TRAIN-derived unless an authorised full MedDRA resource is supplied in a separate experiment.
reportability_boundary: This run is a real-data closed-code evaluation, not a full licensed-MedDRA open-set benchmark.
